supporting_evidence: Elderly patient with weakness and dizziness — dehydration is the most common cause
BP 118/68 is low-normal and consistent with mild volume depletion
Metformin and recent antibiotic course may both contribute to reduced oral intake
Near-fall is consistent with orthostatic symptoms from hypovolemia
Dizziness and near-fall on standing from bed — classic orthostatic presentation
Metoprolol may blunt compensatory heart rate response to postural change
Age 79 with autonomic dysfunction risk from diabetes
Recent UTI treatment 6 days ago — possible treatment failure
Immunosuppressed on methotrexate + prednisone — impaired infection response
E. coli with quinolone resistance — may also be resistant to TMP-SMX
Vague symptoms (weakness, fatigue) can be the only presentation of sepsis in elderly
Methotrexate can cause fatigue, weakness, and bone marrow suppression
Concurrent TMP-SMX increases methotrexate toxicity via reduced renal clearance
Drug interaction between TMP-SMX and methotrexate is well-documented and potentially fatal
CBC with differential needed to assess for pancytopenia
Non-specific symptoms of weakness and fatigue common in viral infections
Immunosuppressed patients are more susceptible to viral illness
Diagnosis of exclusion after ruling out more serious causes

reasoning: 79-year-old female on immunosuppressive therapy presenting with non-specific weakness and dizziness. The differential is broad given the vague presentation. Dehydration and orthostatic hypotension are statistically most likely in this age group. However, urosepsis must be considered given the recent UTI treatment and immunosuppression — this is flagged as a don't-miss diagnosis. Methotrexate toxicity is particularly relevant given the concurrent TMP-SMX course, which is a known dangerous drug interaction. Vital signs are normal, which makes an acute infectious process less likely but does not exclude it in an immunosuppressed patient.
